## NYT::NFlow::TMoniumDriverConfig {#NYT_NFlow_TMoniumDriverConfig}
<!-- This file is generated by yt/yt/flow/tools/generate_yson_struct_doc/generate.sh script -->
<!-- Before using doc generation tool check readme: yt/yt/flow/tools/generate_yson_struct_doc/README.md -->
Source: [yt/yt/flow/yandex/extensions/monium/cpp/spec.h]({{source-root}}/yt/yt/flow/yandex/extensions/monium/cpp/spec.h)

#|
|| **Parameter** | **Description** ||
|| `endpoint` | **Type**: `std::string`
**Required parameter**
Address of the Monium gRPC server (host:port). ||
|| `secure` | **Type**: `bool`
**Default value**: `false`
Use TLS for the connection. ||
|| `compression_algorithm` | **Type**: `std::string`
**Default value**: `none`
 ||
|| `auth_mode` | **Type**: [NYT::NFlow::EMoniumAuthMode](./all_yson_structs#NYT_NFlow_EMoniumAuthMode)
**Default value**: `o_auth_env`
Authentication mode: `Tvm`, `OAuthEnv`, or `Iam`. See also the [Configuring authentication](../release/basic-rules.md#authentication) section. ||
|| `tvm_alias` | **Type**: `std::string`
**Default value**: `monium`
TVM client alias that the driver uses to obtain a ticket in `Tvm` mode. Must match the alias registered in the pipeline's TVM client. ||
|| `oauth_env_var` | **Type**: `std::string`
**Default value**: `MONIUM_TOKEN`
Name of the environment variable containing the OAuth token for `OAuthEnv` mode. Default is `MONIUM_TOKEN`. The pipeline-authenticator sets both `MONIUM_TOKEN` and `SOLOMON_TOKEN` (for backward compatibility) in the worker. ||
|| `iam_metadata_url` | **Type**: `std::string`
**Default value**: `TString("")`
Reserved for future implementation of discovery via the IAM metadata server. Currently, the `Iam` mode only reads the token from the `IAM_TOKEN` environment variable; setting a non-empty value will cause an error when loading the driver. ||
|| `network_thread_count` | **Type**: `int`
**Default value**: `2`
Number of gRPC client threads handling unary driver calls. ||
|| `stop_before_destroying` | **Type**: `bool`
**Default value**: `true`
If `true`, the gRPC pool is stopped before the resource is destroyed (drain in-flight callbacks). Default is `true`. ||
|#
